title: Update working memory from issue 376 identity storage session
summary: Durable context from session 336722ff about main-agent identity and soul storage, fallback sentinel semantics, node service command boundaries, and test isolation.

components.name: Onboarding wizard and identity settings service
Node host service installation and runner

flows.name: Main-agent identity and soul persist through agents/main files with root fallback

claims.text: For the main agent, agents/main/IDENTITY.md and agents/main/SOUL.md are the primary identity and soul files; root IDENTITY.md and SOUL.md remain legacy fallbacks only when the agent-scoped file is absent, because writing root files after agent-scoped files exist causes settings and Gon refreshes to re-read stale data.

load_identity_for_agent("main") checks agents/main/IDENTITY.md by file existence; if the file exists, parsed content or None from an empty sentinel is returned without falling back to root, and save_identity_for_agent writes an empty sentinel when all identity fields are cleared.

load_soul_for_agent("main") checks agents/main/SOUL.md by file existence; if the file exists, content or None from an empty clear file is returned without falling back to root, and save_soul_for_agent writes an empty file for None or empty soul content.

identity_get resolves the main identity through resolve_identity_from_config, which merges config with load_identity_for_agent("main") and sets soul from load_soul_for_agent("main"), so server-injected Gon identity refreshes use the same agent-scoped files that settings updates write.

LiveOnboardingService uses the agent-aware helpers for main-agent onboarding and settings writes: wizard completion and identity_update save IDENTITY.md through save_identity_for_agent("main"), while identity_update and identity_update_soul save SOUL.md through save_soul_for_agent("main").

The terminal onboarding wizard, sandbox container prefix, gateway instance slug, and synthesized main agent persona all read main-agent file identity through load_identity_for_agent("main") rather than the root-only load_identity helper.
Gateway services write agent soul through save_soul_for_agent for every agent id, so the main agent no longer special-cases root save_soul in write_soul_for_agent.

The node host launchd plist and systemd unit run `moltis node run --timeout <seconds>`; gateway URL, device token, node id, display name, and working directory are expected to come from node.json at runtime rather than service-manager CLI flags.

The session rejected adding launchd or systemd command arguments such as --gateway-url, --device-token, --node-id, --name, or --working-dir for `moltis node run`; only --timeout is a valid service-manager CLI override because the rest of ServiceConfig is read from node.json.

Project worktree tests set commit.gpgsign=false in their temporary git repositories before creating the initial empty commit, isolating test branch resolution from a developer's global GPG signing and pinentry configuration.

sources.title: SWE-chat Claude Code session 336722ff: issue 376 identity storage fix

metadata.reason: The session diagnosed issue 376 as root writes being ignored when agents/main files already existed, then kept root files only as legacy fallback while moving main-agent reads and writes to agents/main.

PR review in the session corrected an attempted launchd fix that added unsupported node run flags and explicitly settled on node.json for connection details with only --timeout passed by service installers.
